parameters.name: base
parameters.label: Base Hold
parameters.description: Determines how much of the base to blend into the output at points where the layer has an effect.

parameters.name: match
parameters.label: Match by
parameters.description: Matches channels in the base input with ones in the layer input by either index or name.

parameters.name: quatrot
parameters.label: Quaternion Blend
parameters.description: Allows rotations with the quaternion attribute set to use spherical interpolation to produce smooth rotation blending (set in the Attribute ).

parameters.name: shortrot
parameters.label: Shortest Path Rotation Blending
parameters.description: It better-handles the blending from one set of angles to another, taking into account 0 degrees is 360 degrees.

parameters.name: rotscope
parameters.label: Rotation Scope
parameters.description: Pattern (like *rx *ry *rz) that identifies which channels are rotations that should be handled specially as per the above option.

parameters.name: cyclelen
parameters.label: Cycle Length
parameters.description: Blend 0 degrees to this angle, generally 360.

parameters.name: effect
parameters.label: Effect
parameters.description: If the third input is supplied, the Effect page will be overridden by the third input's first channel, which should contain the effect values over the range of the layer.

parameters.name: relative
parameters.label: Unit Values
parameters.description: Sets the meaning of the next four parameters - either Absolute values, Relative to the Start/End of the channel, or Relative to the Current . The layer and base are never shifted.

parameters.name: start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: The beginning of the composite interval. Effect is zero at the start.

parameters.name: peak
parameters.label: Peak
parameters.description: Where the composite operation reaches maximum effect. This value is held until the release point.

parameters.name: release
parameters.label: Release
parameters.description: The point at which the effect begins to fall back towards zero.

parameters.name: end
parameters.label: End
parameters.description: The end of the composite operation's effect. The effect reduces to zero again.

parameters.name: risefunc
parameters.label: Rise Shape
parameters.description: How to interpolate from one to another. It is the shape of the segment between the Start and Peak indices.

parameters.name: fallfunc
parameters.label: Fall Shape
parameters.description: How to interpolate from one to another. It is the shape of the segment between the Release and End.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page. See .

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).
